<section class="sphx-glr-example-title" id="multi-otsu-thresholding">
<span id="sphx-glr-auto-examples-segmentation-plot-multiotsu-py"></span><h1>Multi-Otsu Thresholding<a class="headerlink" href="#multi-otsu-thresholding" title="Link to this heading">#</a></h1>
<p>The multi-Otsu threshold <a class="footnote-reference brackets" href="#id2" id="id1" role="doc-noteref"><span class="fn-bracket">[</span>1<span class="fn-bracket">]</span></a> is a thresholding algorithm that is used to separate
the pixels of an input image into several different classes, each one obtained
according to the intensity of the gray levels within the image.</p>
<p>Multi-Otsu calculates several thresholds, determined by the number of desired
classes. The default number of classes is 3: for obtaining three classes, the
algorithm returns two threshold values. They are represented by a red line in
the histogram below.</p>
<aside class="footnote-list brackets">
<aside class="footnote brackets" id="id2" role="doc-footnote">
<span class="label"><span class="fn-bracket">[</span><a role="doc-backlink" href="#id1">1</a><span class="fn-bracket">]</span></span>
<p>Liao, P-S., Chen, T-S. and Chung, P-C., “A fast algorithm for multilevel
thresholding”, Journal of Information Science and Engineering 17 (5):
713-727, 2001. Available at:
<<a class="reference external" href="https://ftp.iis.sinica.edu.tw/JISE/2001/200109_01.pdf">https://ftp.iis.sinica.edu.tw/JISE/2001/200109_01.pdf</a>>.</p>
</aside>
</aside>
<img src="../../_images/sphx_glr_plot_multiotsu_001.png" srcset="../../_images/sphx_glr_plot_multiotsu_001.png" alt="Original, Histogram, Multi-Otsu result" class="sphx-glr-single-img"><div class="highlight-Python notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre id="codecell0" tabindex="-1"><span></span><span class="kn">import</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">matplotlib</span>
<span class="kn">import</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">matplotlib.pyplot</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="k">as</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">plt</span>
<span class="kn">import</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">numpy</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="k">as</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">np</span>

<span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">skimage</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">data</span>
<span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">skimage.filters</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <a href="../../api/skimage.filters.html#skimage.filters.threshold_multiotsu" title="skimage.filters.threshold_multiotsu" class="sphx-glr-backref-module-skimage-filters sphx-glr-backref-type-py-function"><span class="n">threshold_multiotsu</span></a>

<span class="c1"># Setting the font size for all plots.</span>
<a href="https://matplotlib.org/stable/api/matplotlib_configuration_api.html#matplotlib.RcParams" title="matplotlib.RcParams" class="sphx-glr-backref-module-matplotlib sphx-glr-backref-type-py-class sphx-glr-backref-instance"><span class="n">matplotlib</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">rcParams</span></a><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'font.size'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="mi">9</span>

<span class="c1"># The input image.</span>
<a href="https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray" title="numpy.ndarray" class="sphx-glr-backref-module-numpy sphx-glr-backref-type-py-class sphx-glr-backref-instance"><span class="n">image</span></a> <span class="o">=</span> <a href="../../api/skimage.data.html#skimage.data.camera" title="skimage.data.camera" class="sphx-glr-backref-module-skimage-data sphx-glr-backref-type-py-function"><span class="n">data</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">camera</span></a><span class="p">()</span>

<span class="c1"># Applying multi-Otsu threshold for the default value, generating</span>
<span class="c1"># three classes.</span>
<a href="https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray" title="numpy.ndarray" class="sphx-glr-backref-module-numpy sphx-glr-backref-type-py-class sphx-glr-backref-instance"><span class="n">thresholds</span></a> <span class="o">=</span> <a href="../../api/skimage.filters.html#skimage.filters.threshold_multiotsu" title="skimage.filters.threshold_multiotsu" class="sphx-glr-backref-module-skimage-filters sphx-glr-backref-type-py-function"><span class="n">threshold_multiotsu</span></a><span class="p">(</span><a href="https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html#numpy.ndarray" title="numpy.ndarray" class="sphx-glr-backref-module-numpy sphx-glr-backref-type-py-class sphx-glr-backref-instance"><span class="n">image</span></a><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Using the threshold values, we generate the three regions.</span>
